user_prompt: are we configuring `mck_io_num` at all anywhere?
agent_reply: No, not in our app code.
- Our real speaker path in [AudioManager.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:819) sets:
so for the NS4168 speaker path we are explicitly not using MCLK / `mck_io_num`.
- Our pin constants only define BCLK, LRCK, DOUT in [pins.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:9).

- `M5.begin(cfg)` in [all_init.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:100) enables `cfg.internal_spk = true`, so M5Unified may build its own temporary speaker config from its board profile. But immediately after begin we call:
so our audio manager later owns I2S directly.

Inside `.pio/libdeps`, M5Unified does assign `pin_mck` for some board profiles, and `Speaker_Class.cpp` maps that to `mck_io_num`, but I don’t see us overriding `pin_mck` anywhere. So if you’re seeing `mck_io_num`, that’s coming from M5Unified’s internal/default speaker config, not from our `AudioManager` speaker setup.

user_prompt: make it configurable in `inc\pins.h`, but leave it unused still, later with the external codec we will need to choose a pin
agent_reply: Done. I added `kNS4168SpeakerMclk = -1` in [inc/pins.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:12), and changed the speaker I2S setup in [AudioManager.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:819) to use that constant.
Because it is `-1`, the behavior stays the same: MCLK is still unused. Later, when the external codec needs it, we can pick a real GPIO in `pins.h`.
One note: the build also left `src/version.c` modified, which looks like the usual generated build/version churn.

commit_message: added potentially useful MCLK pin def

user_prompt: For the M5Stack Core2 as we have, what are all the I2C addresses being used?
agent_reply: Using 7-bit I2C addresses, the Core2 internal bus is SDA `21` / SCL `22` per [inc/pins.h](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:16>).

| `0x34` | AXP192 PMIC | Used by M5Unified and directly by our speaker GPIO2 fallback in [AudioManager.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1018>). Core2 v1.1 AXP2101 is also `0x34`. |
| `0x38` | FT5x06 / FT6336 touch | M5GFX Core2 touch config uses this. |
| `0x51` | BM8563 / PCF8563 RTC | M5Unified RTC default. |

| `0x68` | MPU6886 IMU | Conditional: our code notes the IMU is on a daughterboard and may disappear with another codec board; it probes internal then external I2C in [ImuOrientation.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ImuOrientation/ImuOrientation.cpp:1>). |
Also on our schematic, but not currently implemented in firmware:

| `0x0A` | SGTL5000XNLA3 external audio codec | Schematic connects `CTRL_DATA`/`CTRL_CLK` to the I2C control bus in [hardware/thefly.sch](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/hardware/thefly.sch:2571>). The address comes from the NXP SGTL5000 datasheet: the 20-QFN part uses `0001010(R/W)`, i.e. 7-bit `0x0A`. Our `AudioManager` still says external I2S codec setup is not implemented. |

So if you run an I2C scanner on this exact setup, I’d expect `0x34`, `0x38`, `0x51`, `0x68`, and possibly `0x0A` if the SGTL5000 board is installed and powered. Source for SGTL5000 address: [NXP SGTL5000 datasheet](https://cache.nxp.com/docs/en/data-sheet/SGTL5000.pdf).
Using 7-bit I2C addresses, the Core2 internal bus is SDA `21` / SCL `22` per [inc/pins.h](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:16>).

| `0x34` | AXP192 PMIC | Used by M5Unified and directly by our speaker GPIO2 fallback in [AudioManager.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:1018>). Core2 v1.1 AXP2101 is also `0x34`. |
| `0x38` | FT5x06 / FT6336 touch | M5GFX Core2 touch config uses this. |
| `0x51` | BM8563 / PCF8563 RTC | M5Unified RTC default. |

| `0x68` | MPU6886 IMU | Conditional: our code notes the IMU is on a daughterboard and may disappear with another codec board; it probes internal then external I2C in [ImuOrientation.cpp](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ImuOrientation/ImuOrientation.cpp:1>). |
Also on our schematic, but not currently implemented in firmware:

| `0x0A` | SGTL5000XNLA3 external audio codec | Schematic connects `CTRL_DATA`/`CTRL_CLK` to the I2C control bus in [hardware/thefly.sch](</d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/hardware/thefly.sch:2571>). The address comes from the NXP SGTL5000 datasheet: the 20-QFN part uses `0001010(R/W)`, i.e. 7-bit `0x0A`. Our `AudioManager` still says external I2S codec setup is not implemented. |

So if you run an I2C scanner on this exact setup, I’d expect `0x34`, `0x38`, `0x51`, `0x68`, and possibly `0x0A` if the SGTL5000 board is installed and powered. Source for SGTL5000 address: [NXP SGTL5000 datasheet](https://cache.nxp.com/docs/en/data-sheet/SGTL5000.pdf).

user_prompt: Write for me `lib\ExtCodec\ExtCodec.cpp` and its associated header file. Please follow the guidelines `developer-docs\code-layout-policy.md` as you write the code
There will be only one instance, make it a namespaced module, and it will work tightly with `AudioManager`
The codec is the `SGTL5000`, and I have added the files `lib\ExtCodec\control_sgtl5000.cpp` and `lib\ExtCodec\control_sgtl5000.h` to help this effort get started.

There will be a initialization function. This will first check the I2C address to see if it responds, and the rest of `AudioManager` and `RecordingView`behavior will depend on if it is available. This is to be done while the splash screen is still happening. Later we might even update the splash screen to indicate if an external CODEC is connected.
there should be a state enum like
GPIO35 is an ADC capable pin and is dedicated to sensing if a earbud is connected, a high-ish voltage means connected
GPIO36 is an ADC capable pin and is dedicated to sensing if a earbud has an inline mic, a low-ish voltage would indicate there’s no mic (as it will be shorted to HP-GND physically)

If the external codec is connected, then we will start up a dedicated ADC thread. It doesn’t need much stack. All it does is do ADC reads once every 100ms, blocking yet yielding. It sets the state of the module accordingly and maybe set an event flag. Other threads will use the state change to reconfigure electrically and reconfigure the GUI accordingly
Another mic is connected to line-in-right of the `SGTL5000` and it will be used if the earbud-inline-mic is not available
Remember that the `SGTL5000` will use stereo and a different BCLK pin for the I2S. The entire I2S does need to be reconfigured when we are switching between the SGTL5000 and the NS4168.
So in our current implementation, we are already operating under `EXTCODEC_UNAVAIL`. No change in behavior needed

Under state `EXTCODEC_NO_EARBUD` we still have push-to-talk behavior, but instead of switching to the `SPM1423` when we push-to-talk, we switch to the `SGTL5000` using line-in-right as the sound source.

Under `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD` we have bidirectional audio, the mic is using line-in-right, do not use push-to-talk behavior, we can mute the mic, we can mute the sound output, but now changing mic state doesn’t change sound output state, and changing sound output state doesn’t change mic state

Under `EXTCODEC_YES_EARBUD_WITH_MIC` we have bidirectional audio, the mic is using the `SGTL5000`'s dedicated mic input, do not use push-to-talk behavior, we can mute the mic, we can mute the sound output, but now changing mic state doesn’t change sound output state, and changing sound output state doesn’t change mic state

Add to `inc\pins.h`, the I2S_BCLK pin for the SGTL5000 will be GPIO13, the I2S_MCLK pin will be GPIO14 (and only used if the external codec is detected). GPIO34 is for the I2S data for the mic. GPIO2 is for the I2S data for the earbud. GPIO0 is for the I2S_LRCLK.
`AudioFifo` already has special enqueue and dequeue functions for handling stereo data, if these are missing then implement them
agent_reply: Created [ExtCodec.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.h:1) and [ExtCodec.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/ExtCodec.cpp:1) as a namespaced singleton-style module for SGTL5000 detection/state. It checks I2C address `0x0A`, initializes SGTL5000 control if present, starts a small ADC monitor task, tracks the four requested states, exposes state/event helpers, and has analog-path helpers for line-in-right vs dedicated mic.

Also added the SGTL5000/I2S/sense pins in [pins.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:16), added tiny compatibility shims [AudioControl.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/AudioControl.h:1) and [AudioStream.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/ExtCodec/AudioStream.h:1) so the starter SGTL5000 control files build, and call `ExtCodec::init()` during splash boot in [main.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:126).

I intentionally did not switch `AudioManager` into external-I2S routing yet, since its external I2S path is still stubbed and you asked current `EXTCODEC_UNAVAIL` behavior to remain unchanged. `AudioFifo` already had stereo enqueue/dequeue functions, so I left that alone.

user_prompt: is the I2S using a stereo or mono setting when using the NS4168? I am asking because it seems to use a LRCLK but it's a mono speaker
agent_reply: For the NS4168 path, the code configures I2S as **mono**:

That is normal. I2S still has a word-select/LRCLK signal even when the data payload is mono; it is part of the bus timing/framing. The mono/stereo behavior is controlled by the slot config and how many samples we write.
The software path also treats NS4168 as mono:
And `speaker_bytes_per_frame()` is one `int16_t` for NS4168, two `int16_t`s for external stereo codec. So: **mono data, with LRCLK still present for I2S framing.**
For the NS4168 path, the code configures I2S as **mono**:

That is normal. I2S still has a word-select/LRCLK signal even when the data payload is mono; it is part of the bus timing/framing. The mono/stereo behavior is controlled by the slot config and how many samples we write.
The software path also treats NS4168 as mono:
And `speaker_bytes_per_frame()` is one `int16_t` for NS4168, two `int16_t`s for external stereo codec. So: **mono data, with LRCLK still present for I2S framing.**

user_prompt: I got a problem here, since that pin is connected internally to the USB serial converter, the MCLK signal is in contention with the TX of the converter
I can switch to the TXD0 pin but I think I would lose the ability to output serial port data
which isn't the end of the world but it makes debugging a lot harder
GPIO0 is already used for the mic's data
Switch the pin to use GPIO1, and edit `bool configure_i2s_shared()` to explicitly disconnect GPIO1 from UART
agent_reply: I changed [pins.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:17) so SGTL5000 MCLK is now GPIO1:

In [AudioManager.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:899), I added `disconnect_uart0_tx_from_mclk_pin()`. It flushes Serial, switches GPIO1 away from UART/IOMUX use, and disconnects the UART output signal before I2S config asks ESP-IDF to start MCLK on that pin.
I call that helper from both MCLK users: `configure_i2s_shared()` and `ensure_exti2scodec_i2s()`, since both can claim GPIO1 for SGTL5000 MCLK.

I also updated the boot comment in [all_init.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/all_init.cpp:62) to reflect that GPIO1 may be reclaimed for MCLK. Practical result: Serial output can work up until shared/external I2S starts, then GPIO1 becomes MCLK and serial debug output on TXD0 is gone.
Validation: `pio run -e thefly_sec0` passed. `src/version.c` was restored after the build.

user_prompt: find me documentation or comments that mention how MCLK and UART conflict
agent_reply: > using the LEDC PWM generator is sufficient to generate MCLK ... leaves the UART0 pins unoccupied.
Serial.begin(115200, SERIAL_8N1, -1, 1); // RXD0/GPIO3 unused; TXD0/GPIO1 may be reclaimed for SGTL5000 MCLK.
The actual conflict-handling code is in [AudioManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:801):
That function only does work if `kSGTL5000I2sMclk == 1`, meaning SGTL5000 MCLK is on GPIO1, which is UART0 TX.
The I2S setup shows the two paths in [AudioManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:948):
Then [AudioManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:958) either starts LEDC MCLK or disconnects UART TX:
And one test prints the behavior explicitly in [src/tests/test_i2cscan.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/tests/test_i2cscan.cpp:130):
enabling shared I2S MCLK on GPIO%d; UART TX is disconnected during the scan
So the short version: the documented conflict is SGTL5000 I2S MCLK on GPIO1 versus UART0 TX on GPIO1. The LEDC path moves MCLK to GPIO14, which avoids stealing UART0 TX.

The I2S setup shows the two paths in [AudioManager.cpp](d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/AudioManager/AudioManager.cpp:948):

So the short version: the documented conflict is SGTL5000 I2S MCLK on GPIO1 versus UART0 TX on GPIO1. The LEDC path moves MCLK to GPIO14, which avoids stealing UART0 TX.

user_prompt: so it initialized fine, and the earbud_adc is still stuck at 4095
these boards are easy to swap and I swapped in a working one, and it, well, works.
are you able to look at the schematic?
just to sanity check your ability, what is the purpose of R1, R2, and R9?
agent_reply: Yes. I looked at [hardware/thefly.sch](/D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/hardware/thefly.sch:2299) and the local ExtCodec notes.
- `R1` is `2K2`: mic-bias feed from SGTL5000 `MIC_BIAS` to `HP-MIC`. It biases an inline/electret mic; the mic signal is then AC-coupled through `C7` into the codec mic input.
- `R2` is `1M`: weak pull-up from `VDDA` to the `MIC_BIAS` node. It helps define/bias that inline-mic detect/mic-bias network.

- `R9` is `1M`: pull-up from `HP-SENSE-L` to `3.3V`. `HP-SENSE-L` goes to the jack detect contact and to the earbud ADC path, which firmware reads on GPIO35 in [pins.h](/D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/pins.h:24).

So for your stuck `4095`: that is very plausibly not codec init. `R9` is literally supposed to pull the earbud sense line high; the jack/sense contact should change that when unplugged. I’d suspect the jack sense switch/contact, solder on `J1` pin 5 or related contact, continuity from `HP-SENSE-L`, or an accidental short/leak to `3.3V`.
